items.name: ASUS Vivobook 16
items.desc: Primary laptop for development — lightweight enough to carry, capable enough for everything I throw at it.

items.name: Custom PC
items.desc: Home rig with 16GB RAM, Intel processor, and AMD Radeon GPU — handles heavier workloads, compiling, and the occasional rendering session.

items.name: Samsung External Monitor
items.desc: Extends the workspace for a more comfortable dual-screen setup when at the desk.

items.name: Full Mechanical Wireless Keyboard
items.desc: Creamy tactile switches with dedicated macro buttons — satisfying to type on and practical for repetitive workflows.

items.name: Wireless Gaming Mouse
items.desc: Precise and lag-free — doubles just as well as a productivity mouse for day-to-day use.

items.name: Antigravity IDE
items.desc: My primary editor for personal projects — an AI-native IDE that integrates agentic workflows directly into the coding experience.

items.name: VS Code
items.desc: Reliable fallback editor, heavily customized with extensions, themes, and keybindings.

items.name: Cursor
items.desc: AI-powered editor I use for work — great for navigating large codebases and getting context-aware suggestions quickly.

items.name: Node.js + Express
items.desc: Go-to backend stack for personal REST APIs and side projects.

items.name: Laravel
items.desc: PHP framework for personal projects — elegant, opinionated, and batteries-included. Great for rapid backend development.

items.name: Next.js
items.desc: Primary frontend framework of choice — full-stack capability, great DX, and excellent SEO support out of the box.

items.name: React + Vite
items.desc: Lightweight alternative for SPAs when a full Next.js setup isn't necessary.

items.name: MongoDB + Mongoose
items.desc: Flexible NoSQL solution for personal projects with dynamic or evolving schemas.

items.name: MySQL / MariaDB
items.desc: Relational database for structured, relation-heavy data — primarily used in work projects.

items.name: Git + GitHub / Bitbucket
items.desc: Git for version control everywhere. GitHub for personal projects, Bitbucket for work repositories.

items.name: Laragon
items.desc: Local Apache + MySQL development server for PHP and Laravel projects on Windows — zero-config and fast.

items.name: Docker + WSL (Ubuntu) — WIP
items.desc: Building a containerized, Linux-first development environment on Windows. Still a work in progress, but the goal is full reproducibility across machines.

items.name: Figma
items.desc: UI/UX design and interactive prototyping for web projects.

items.name: GIMP
items.desc: Open-source image editor for asset preparation, photo manipulation, and quick edits.

items.name: Canva
items.desc: Fast graphic design for social content, presentations, and non-technical visuals.

items.name: Blender
items.desc: Dabbled in 3D modeling — exploring it as a creative outlet when time allows.

items.name: Cloudinary
items.desc: Cloud-based image hosting and on-the-fly transformation for web projects.

items.name: Render
items.desc: Hosts my Node.js/Express APIs — works well on the free tier, even with the occasional cold start.

items.name: Vercel
items.desc: Deploys React and Next.js apps with zero config, instant preview URLs, and seamless CI/CD from GitHub.

items.name: GitHub Pages
items.desc: Serves purely static HTML/CSS/JS projects — simple, free, and perfectly reliable for what it does.

items.name: GoDaddy
items.desc: Where I register and manage custom domains for my projects.

items.name: Cloudflare
items.desc: DNS and CDN layer that connects custom domains to Vercel and GitHub Pages deployments, with added performance and security.

items.name: Antigravity
items.desc: My primary AI agent for personal projects — blends coding, image generation, and planning into a single agentic workflow.

items.name: Claude
items.desc: Preferred AI for code review, refactoring, architecture discussions, and anything that needs careful reasoning.

items.name: ChatGPT
items.desc: General-purpose AI for brainstorming, quick drafts, and everyday queries.

items.name: Gemini
items.desc: Go-to for image-related tasks — understanding, describing, and generating visuals.

items.name: YouTube
items.desc: Primary source for tutorials, conference talks, and deep-dives into new technology.

items.name: freeCodeCamp
items.desc: The structured curriculum that laid the foundation of my development journey.

items.name: GeeksforGeeks
items.desc: Quick reference for data structures, algorithms, and language-specific concepts.

items.name: Udemy
items.desc: Project-based courses for picking up new frameworks and technologies at my own pace.

items.name: Coursera
items.desc: Formal learning paths and certifications from universities and top tech companies.

items.name: Google Courses
items.desc: Google's free certification programs — useful for cloud, analytics, and career development.

items.name: KodeKloud
items.desc: Hands-on labs for DevOps, Docker, Kubernetes, and Linux fundamentals with a learn-by-doing approach.
